response:
[SOphia HAS TWO SCIENCE TESTS, BIOLOGY AND CHEMISTRY, WITH 25 AND 20 QUESTIONS, RESPECTIVELY. SHE HAS 45 MINUTES TO COMPLETE THE BIOLOGY TEST AND 1 HOUR TO COMPLETE THE CHEMISTRY TEST. IF SOPHIA WANTS TO ALLOCATE THE SAME AMOUNT OF TIME FOR EACH QUESTION, HOW MANY MORE SECONDS CAN SHE SPEND ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST?]

[HOW MANY MORE SECONDS CAN SHE SPEND ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST?]

[THE TIME PER QUESTION FOR BIOLOGY IS 45 MINUTES DIVIDED BY 25 QUESTIONS, WHICH IS 45*60/25 SECONDS PER QUESTION. THE TIME PER QUESTION FOR CHEMISTRY IS 1 HOUR DIVIDED BY 20 QUESTIONS, WHICH IS 60*60/20 SECONDS PER QUESTION. THE DIFFERENCE IS (60*60/20) - (45*60/25) SECONDS.]

[THE DIFFERENCE IS 108 - 108 = 0 SECONDS.]

[HOW MANY MORE SECONDS CAN SHE SPEND ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST?]

[THE DIFFERENCE IS 0 SECONDS.]
